seo.title: S. Carolina Dental Assistant License (2026) | Fees & Guide
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant requirements in South Carolina — fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license south carolina, dental assistant certification south carolina, RDA south carolina, DANB CDA south carolina, dental assistant radiography south carolina

board.name: South Carolina State Board of Dentistry
board.mailing_address: SC Board of Dentistry, Post Office Box 11329, Columbia, SC 29211-1329

license_types.title: Dental Assistant (no state license required)
license_types.description: No state-level credential required for general dental assistants in South Carolina.

steps.title: Create Account and Submit Online Application
steps.description: Register for an account on the SC LLR Online Services Portal and complete the Expanded Duty Dental Assistant (EDDA) application form, ensuring all required fields are accurately filled.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Remit the non-refundable application fee of $75 through the SC LLR Online Services Portal during the application submission process.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from a CODA-accredited dental assisting program or a Board-approved Expanded Duty Dental Assisting course to be sent directly to the South Carolina Board of Dentistry.

steps.title: Provide BLS Certification
steps.description: Upload a clear copy of your current Basic Life Support (BLS) certification card to your online application or submit it directly to the Board.

steps.title: Submit Proof of Age
steps.description: Provide a copy of a government-issued photo identification (e.g., driver's license) that clearly shows your date of birth, either by uploading it to the portal or submitting it separately.

steps.title: Pass Board-Approved Examination
steps.description: Submit proof of passing a Board-approved Expanded Duty Dental Assisting examination, such as the South Carolina Dental Association (SCDA) examination or other Board-approved equivalent.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: South Carolina does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. DANB national certification is recognized and recommended.

faqs.question: Does South Carolina require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: No, South Carolina does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. However, expanded functions and radiography may require permits or certifications.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in South Carolina?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in South Carolina.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in South Carolina?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in South Carolina?
faqs.answer: There is no state application fee for general dental assistants in South Carolina. However, DANB CDA exam costs $450, and radiography certifications may have additional fees.

reciprocity.notes: South Carolina does not license general dental assistants. Expanded duty training required for specific procedures.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in South Carolina
hero.dek: To apply for an Expanded Duty Dental Assistant (EDDA) license in South Carolina, you must submit your application and all required supporting documentation through the SC LLR Online Services Portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the SC LLR Online Services Portal. Click 'Register' to create a new account by providing personal details (Name, SSN/ITIN, Date of Birth, Email). Once registered, log in and select 'Apply for a New License/Registration' under the Dentistry Board, then choose 'Expanded Duty Dental Assistant (EDDA)'.
